title: Power BI Data Modeling for Security Snapshots — Star Schema, Unpivoting, Period Combination
applies_to: Unpivoting wide CSV to tall format

confirmed_facts.fact: Star schema for security snapshots: one fact table (SecuritySnapshots — one row per period per metric) with dimensions DateTable and MetricDim. Relationships radiate from fact to dimensions, single cross-filter direction.

Fact table grain: one row per period per metric with raw value only. Never store MoM delta or % change as columns — compute in DAX. Storing pre-computed deltas creates circular dependencies and breaks time intelligence.

Wide format (one column per metric) cannot use a MetricDim dimension. Tall format (one row per metric per period) enables: filter by metric name, sort by SortOrder, per-metric thresholds, single set of DAX measures for all metrics.
Table.UnpivotOtherColumns keeps fixed columns (Date, SourceFile) and pivots everything else into rows. More robust than Table.Unpivot when new metric columns appear in newer files.

MetricDim dimension stores per-metric thresholds (RedThreshold, AmberThreshold), LowIsGood flag, SortOrder, and category. SELECTEDVALUE(MetricDim[RedThreshold]) works in matrix row context to drive dynamic RAG formatting.
Monthly and quarterly snapshots can coexist in one fact table with a PeriodType column ('Monthly'/'Quarterly'). Quarterly snapshots use first month of quarter as YearMonthKey: Q1=202601, Q2=202604.

implementation_notes:
Fact table schema:
- SnapshotDate (date, first of period)
- MetricID (integer FK to MetricDim)
- Value (integer or decimal, the raw count/score)
- PeriodType (text: 'Monthly' or 'Quarterly')
- SourceFile (text, original filename)
- YearMonthKey (integer YYYYMM, join key to DateTable)

MetricDim as inline Power Query table:
```m
MetricDim = Table.FromRows({
    {1, "Sev5 Vulns",  "Vulnerability", 1, 100, 50, true,  "Count"},
    {2, "Sev4 Vulns",  "Vulnerability", 2, 200, 100, true, "Count"},
    {3, "External",    "Exposure",      3, 30,  15,  true, "Count"},
    {4, "Internal",    "Exposure",      4, 50,  25,  true, "Count"},
    {5, "Open Alerts", "Alert",         5, 20,  10,  true, "Count"}
},
{"MetricID","MetricName","MetricCategory","SortOrder",
 "RedThreshold","AmberThreshold","LowIsGood","Unit"})
```

Unpivot wide CSV to tall fact table:
```m
let
    Source = SharePoint.Files("https://contoso.sharepoint.com/sites/Security", [ApiVersion=15]),
    Filtered = Table.SelectRows(Source, each Text.Contains([Folder Path], "/Reports/Monthly/")),
    CSVOnly = Table.SelectRows(Filtered, each [Extension] = ".csv"),
    AddFile = Table.AddColumn(CSVOnly, "SourceFile", each [Name], type text),
    Parsed = Table.AddColumn(AddFile, "Data",
        each Table.PromoteHeaders(Csv.Document([Content], [Delimiter=",", Encoding=65001]))),
    Expanded = Table.ExpandTableColumn(Parsed, "Data", Table.ColumnNames(Parsed{0}[Data])),
    Cleaned = Table.RemoveColumns(Expanded,
        {"Content","Extension","Date accessed","Date modified","Date created","Attributes","Folder Path"}),
    TypedDate = Table.TransformColumnTypes(Cleaned, {{"Date", type date}}),
    // UNPIVOT: keep Date and SourceFile fixed, pivot everything else
    Unpivoted = Table.UnpivotOtherColumns(TypedDate, {"Date", "SourceFile"}, "MetricName", "Value"),
    TypedValue = Table.TransformColumnTypes(Unpivoted, {{"Value", Int64.Type}}),
    AddKey = Table.AddColumn(TypedValue, "YearMonthKey",
        each Date.Year([Date]) * 100 + Date.Month([Date]), Int64.Type)
in
    AddKey
```

DAX measures for the 'this month vs change' pattern:
```dax
// One set of measures works for ALL metrics when MetricDim[MetricName] is on matrix rows
Metric Value = SUM(SecuritySnapshots[Value])

Metric PM = CALCULATE([Metric Value], PREVIOUSMONTH('DateTable'[Date]))

Metric MoM Change =
    VAR _C = [Metric Value]
    VAR _P = [Metric PM]
    RETURN IF(NOT ISBLANK(_C) && NOT ISBLANK(_P), _C - _P, BLANK())

Metric MoM % =
    VAR _C = [Metric Value]
    VAR _P = [Metric PM]
    RETURN IF(ISBLANK(_P) || _P = 0, BLANK(), DIVIDE(_C - _P, ABS(_P)))

Metric LY = CALCULATE([Metric Value], SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('DateTable'[Date]))

Metric YoY % =
    VAR _C = [Metric Value]
    VAR _L = [Metric LY]
    RETURN IF(ISBLANK(_L) || _L = 0, BLANK(), DIVIDE(_C - _L, ABS(_L)))
```

Dynamic RAG using per-metric thresholds:
```dax
RAG Status =
VAR _Val   = [Metric Value]
VAR _Red   = SELECTEDVALUE(MetricDim[RedThreshold], 100)
VAR _Amber = SELECTEDVALUE(MetricDim[AmberThreshold], 50)
RETURN
    IF(ISBLANK(_Val), BLANK(),
    SWITCH(TRUE(), _Val >= _Red, 2, _Val >= _Amber, 1, 0))
// 2=Red, 1=Amber, 0=Green — use with icon conditional formatting
```

Combining monthly + quarterly snapshots:
```m
// Load monthly → unpivot → add PeriodType = "Monthly"
// Load quarterly → unpivot → add PeriodType = "Quarterly"
SecuritySnapshots = Table.Combine({MonthlyFact, QuarterlyFact})
```

DAX to scope quarterly-only metrics:
```dax
Quarterly Score = CALCULATE([Metric Value], SecuritySnapshots[PeriodType] = "Quarterly")
```

Excel source variant (instead of CSV):
```m
// Replace Csv.Document with Excel.Workbook:
ParseExcel = Table.AddColumn(FilteredFiles, "Data",
    each let
        wb = Excel.Workbook([Content], true, true),
        sheet = wb{[Kind="Sheet", Name="Data"]}[Data]
    in sheet)
// Or reference named table: wb{[Kind="Table", Name="SecurityData"]}[Data]
```
caveats: Never store pre-computed MoM deltas in the fact table — breaks DAX time intelligence and creates maintenance burden
Wide format fact table (columns=metrics) makes it impossible to add new metrics without schema changes
Normalise SnapshotDate to first of month — dates like 2026-03-15 won't join correctly to DateTable where the match is 2026-03-01
Always include PeriodType column when mixing monthly and quarterly snapshots — otherwise measures double-count
Use Table.UnpivotOtherColumns (not Table.Unpivot with fixed names) to handle new metrics appearing in future files
MetricDim must be loaded into the data model with a relationship to the fact table — not just a loose Power Query table
Quarterly Q2 2026 must use YearMonthKey=202604 (April start), not 202606 (June end)
